records.quote: Quarterly revenue of $2.99 billion came in below the Street estimate of $3.13 billion.

Elon Musk Congratulates Apple As Tim Cook Marks 50-Year Milestone Apple CEO Tim Cook celebrated the company's upcoming 50th anniversary by reflecting on its innovation and impact. Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL ) is set to mark its 50th anniversary on April 1. On Thursday, Apple CEO Tim Cook thanked everyone, drawing a reaction from Elon Musk. Tim Cook Looks Ahead To Apple's Golden Milestone In a blog post that was shared on X, Cook reflected on Apple's journey from a small garage startup to one of the world's most influential technology companies. "Fifty years ago in a small garage, a big idea was born. Apple was founded on the simple notion that technology should be personal, and that belief — radical at the time — changed everything," he said. Cook highlighted Apple's iconic products, from the original Apple computer and Mac to the iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch and AirPods, as well as digital services like the App Store, Apple Music, Apple Pay, iCloud and Apple TV. He said that the real impact comes from users: "Every invention we bring into the world is just the beginning of a story. The most meaningful chapters are written by all of you — the people who use our technology to work, learn, dream, and discover." Congratulations — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) March 12, 2026 Elon Musk Congratulates Apple Musk responded to Cook's post with a brief message, saying, "Congratulations." Congratulations — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) March 12, 2026 Apple's Record-Breaking Performance Apple currently has a market capitalization of $3.76 trillion. In its fiscal first quarter of 2026, the company reported revenue of $143.76 billion, beating analyst estimates of $138.42 billion, with earnings of $2.84 per share, ahead of forecasts of $2.66. Product revenue totaled $113.74 billion, up from $97.96 billion year-over-year, while services revenue reached $30.01 billion, up from $26.34 billion. Price Action: Apple closed at $255.76 on Thursday, down 1.94%. In pre-market trading, at the time of writing, it is showing a slight dip to $255.38, down 0.15%, according to Benzinga Pro. According to Benzinga Edge Stock Rankings, AAPL shares are showing a downward trend across short, medium and long-term periods, even though the company's Quality score ranks in the 94th percentile. AbbVie Inc. (NYSE: ABBV ) reported third-quarter 2025 sales of $15.78 billion on Friday, beating the consensus of $15.59 billion.
Quarterly revenue came in at $4.09 billion, which beat the Street estimate of $4.07 billion.
The company reported sales of $18.04 billion, slightly exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $17.84 billion.
